LexTransition AI is an open-source, offline-first legal assistant. It helps users navigate the transition from old Indian laws (IPC/CrPC/IEA) to the new **BNS/BNSS/BSA** frameworks. Using local Machine Learning and OCR, it analyzes legal documents and maps law sections with 100% grounded accuracy.

Fully automated and interpretable framework for motion phenotype classification from high-speed microscopy videos. Provides reproducible feature extraction and hierarchical machine learning tools for biomedical video analysis.

The best-performing model was identified through error metric analysis and subsequently hybridized with machine learning models to capture both linear and non-linear patterns in financial data. The project integrates data engineering, advanced machine-learning models, hyperparameter optimisation, and algorithmic trading mechanisms.

A Python-based framework designed to quantify risk in Bitcoin trading using Probabilistic Machine Learning. Most ML models fail in crypto because they ignore oratorical uncertainty (the inherent randomness of the market). This repo provides tools to build models that don't just predict the next move

A phishing detection framework that utilizes machine learning, adversarial training, and multi-modal feature extraction. It analyzes various data sources, including URLs, HTML, metadata, and user behavior, while leveraging GAN-generated samples to protect against adversarial attacks.

A machine learningbased flood risk prediction system that utilizes open geospatial and meteorological data to identify urban flood-prone zones. By integrating GIS processing, digital elevation models, rainfall data, and feature engineering techniques, the project aims to develop a cost-effective and scalable predictive framework.

This is a repository for Quasi-Analytic Physics-Informed Machine Learning program for solving inverse problems of consolidation (i.e., diffusion PDE). The framework is designed to address field heterogeneity and measurement sparsity, which are major concerns for site characterization tasks in geotechnical engineering.

An open-source framework for machine learning and other computations on decentralized data.

A multi-level obfuscation framework (mild, moderate, strong) for privacy-preserving machine learning on network-traffic data. Includes dataset balancing, obfuscation scripts, TabNet models, baseline ML benchmarks, and reproducible experiments.

Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) framework featuring comprehensive model interpretability using SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations). This repository showcases the end-to-end process of automated feature engineering, hyperparameter optimization, and the use of game-theory-based explanations to visualize global and local feature importance.

A growing collection of fundamental Machine Learning algorithms implemented completely from scratch using Python and `numpy`. This repository serves as an educational resource to understand the inner workings of various ML models without relying on high-level frameworks like scikit-learn for the core logic.
Machine LearningSHAPSCAPS framework for solar cell optimization

This project presents a physics-inspired spacecraft telemetry simulation and anomaly detection framework that models power, thermal, and communication subsystems in Low Earth Orbit. It combines engineering-based thresholds and machine learning to analyze synthetic telemetry and study fault detection and spacecraft health monitoring.

Replication code for "A Term Structure Framework for Green Bond Spreads and Portfolio Strategies." Includes R scripts for curve-based green bond yield spread calculation, machine learning-based attribute screening via Association Rule Learning, and green bond portfolio construction and evaluation.

Course project for the Machine Learning (Advanced) class at CEU. This repository implements a simple AutoML framework for regression using simulated annealing to select models and tune hyperparameters, with different temperature multipliers for model and hyperparameter changes. The approach is compared with TPOT and auto-sklearn.

This project presents a dynamic, unsupervised machine learning framework for predicting wildfire risk using multivariate weather time-series data. Unlike traditional static wildfire danger indices, this system adapts to temporal variations in environmental conditions using context-based modeling and anomaly detection.

QueryInsights is a learning-to-rank system that demonstrates how search results can be ordered using machine learning. It compares logistic regression and XGBoost models, evaluates ranking quality with NDCG@10, and simulates potential click-through improvements using an offline A/B framework.
